title: Multipart Upload State, Parts, Checksums, and Cleanup · Go Semantics Case: Abort Lifecycle Cleanup
summary: A worker starts multipart uploads without durable state, chooses tiny parts, loses ETags, retries concurrent parts blindly, and never aborts abandoned uploads. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the abort lifecycle cleanup increment in recoverable multipart-upload state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Abort known failures, inventory abandoned uploads, and configure AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload lifecycle cleanup where supported.
Validate ordered parts, complete once, and reconcile timeout or connection-loss ambiguity with object metadata and upload state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Abort Lifecycle Cleanup · Theory Abort Lifecycle Cleanup
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6986 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate multipart part state, retained ETag evidence, cancellation, and abort cleanup; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Abort known failures, inventory abandoned uploads, and configure AbortIncompleteMultipartUpload lifecycle cleanup where supported.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Abort Lifecycle Cleanup contract for the supplied data.
